species: Lyme disease is a common tick-borne infection caused by the spirochete Borrelia burgdorferi sensu stricto (s.s.).
tissue: Salp12 is expressed in the midguts and salivary glands of I. scapularis ticks.
disease_state: Lyme disease is a common tick-borne infection caused by the spirochete Borrelia burgdorferi sensu stricto (s.s.).
sample_source: To gain a better understanding of Salp12 expression and its role within the tick, we assessed salp12 mRNA expression during the course of feeding in I. scapularis nymphs.
age: For all mouse experiments, female, 5–10 weeks of age, C3H/HEJ mice were used.
cell_line: Schneider’s Drosophila Line 2 cells purchased from ATCC (CVCL_Z232) were used for protein expression and purification.
sex: For all mouse experiments, female, 5–10 weeks of age, C3H/HEJ mice were used.
strain: For all mouse experiments, female, 5–10 weeks of age, C3H/HEJ mice were used.
